games.explanation: The market is pricing Luinder Avila like a 56-batter sample is real data, and it isn't. He's a rookie who's faced fewer hitters than most relievers see in a month, and the Nationals send a lefty-heavy top of the order led by James Wood, who's been punishing right-handed pitching all year to the tune of a .370 xwOBA. Littell isn't a star, but he's a strike-thrower who lets his defense work, and that's a perfectly acceptable counter against a Kansas City lineup that thins out quickly after Witt and Pasquantino. The better team is getting plus-money because of a shiny ERA built on three starts. At -130, you're paying fair freight on the club that should actually win this game.

Sandy Alcantara's name still carries weight on the betting board, but the version pitching this season is a different animal, especially when lefties step in. Philly happens to roll out a lineup built to exploit exactly that, with Schwarber, Harper, and Marsh all swinging from the left side against an arm that's coughed up a .351 xwOBA to that handedness. The Phillies are the better team by a wide margin here, and a near coin-flip price on the Elo favorite at home is the kind of thing the market should not be offering. Take the chalk and let Schwarber do the rest.
ATL is the better team by Elo and the sim confirms it — the market isn't giving them enough credit. Grant Holmes toes the rubber against Adrian Houser. ATL holds a commanding 119-point Elo edge.

Robbie Ray's strikeout numbers paper over the fact that he is walking the park and getting barreled up by right-handed bats, which is precisely the wrong matchup for a Braves lineup that runs Olson, Baldwin, and Albies at him. Righties are tagging Ray for a .306 xwOBA with a hard-hit rate pushing 48%, and Atlanta happens to own the kind of damage profile that turns his command wobbles into crooked numbers. Pair that with the better team by a clear Elo margin and you've got a favorite the market is pricing like a coin flip. -125 on the side with the lineup edge and the rotation edge is the cleaner read.
